steps.text: You made bar graphs with scale of 10. You read values at the lines and between the lines. Let's see what you know.

incorrects.description: Student selected 4: counted bars instead of reading the axis value

params.description: The 40 line on the axis pulses to draw attention

steps.text: Let's think about this together. We don't count the bars. We look at where the Fiction bar ends on the axis.

incorrects.description: Student selected 35: estimated between axis lines instead of reading the exact value

params.description: The 40 line pulses, showing Fiction bar ends exactly at it

steps.text: Here's a hint: the Fiction bar ends exactly at an axis line. Look at which number that line shows.

incorrects.description: Student selected 45: read the wrong axis line or estimated incorrectly

params.description: Top of Fiction bar highlights, then line traces to axis

steps.text: You're working on it. Follow the top of the Fiction bar across to the axis. Which line does it touch?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Line draws from top of Fiction bar horizontally to the 40 line on axis, which highlights

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I look at where the top of the Fiction bar is. Then I trace across to the axis. The bar ends right at the line marked 40. So Fiction shows 40.

incorrects.description: Student selected 30: read the lower gridline instead of finding the bar's endpoint

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The bar doesn't stop at 30. It goes higher. Look at where the top of the bar ends.

incorrects.description: Student selected 34: attempted to estimate but missed that the bar is exactly halfway

steps.text: You're working on it. The bar is exactly halfway between 30 and 40. What number is halfway between them?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40: read the upper gridline instead of finding the bar's endpoint

steps.text: Here's a hint: the bar doesn't reach 40. It stops between the two lines. Where does the top of the bar end?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Ghost gridlines at 30 and 40 pulse. Bar endpoint highlighted at exact midpoint. Value 35 appears.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The bar is between 30 and 40. It's exactly in the middle. Halfway between 30 and 40 is 35. The bar shows 35 points.

incorrects.description: Student set at least one bar incorrectly on first attempt

steps.text: Check the data table. Some values land on the lines, others fall between.

incorrects.description: Student set at least one bar incorrectly on second attempt

steps.text: Here's a hint: Markers is 30, right at the third line. Crayons is 45, halfway between 40 and 50.

incorrects.description: Student set at least one bar incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: All bars fill to correct heights: Markers=30, Crayons=45, Paintbrushes=20, Glue Sticks=55

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Markers is 30, at the line. Crayons is 45, between 40 and 50. Paintbrushes is 20, at the line. Glue Sticks is 55, between 50 and 60.

incorrects.description: Student selected 15: confused the difference between Crayons (45) and Markers (30) instead of Glue Sticks and Markers

steps.text: Look at the Glue Sticks bar and the Markers bar. We need to find the difference between those two, not other categories.

incorrects.description: Student selected 55: chose the height of Glue Sticks instead of finding the difference

steps.text: You found how many Glue Sticks there are. The question asks how many more, so we need to find the difference between the two bars.

incorrects.description: Student selected 85: added Glue Sticks (55) and Markers (30) instead of finding the difference

steps.text: Here's a hint: the word more tells us to compare. We need to subtract to find how many more, not add the two amounts together.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Visual shows Glue Sticks bar at 55, Markers bar at 30, with bracket or arrow showing the gap of 25 between them

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Glue Sticks reaches 55. Markers reaches 30. The difference between 55 and 30 is 25. The answer is 25.
You can read bar graphs with scale of 10, even values between the lines. You're ready to practice.
